stat_of_week: 52 - The Sugar Land Skeeters scored 52 runs in a single week, the most explosive offensive output of the season and nearly double the league average, fueled by eight players reaching at least 3 runs scored.
thriller_note: A razor-thin tie where General Hospital's elite rotation (Wrobleski's 0.69 ERA, Fried's 0.63 WHIP, 5 QS) was neutralized by Hiroshima's 12 NSVH and McLean's 17 strikeouts, leaving both teams frustrated and setting up a standings logjam in the middle of the pack.
key_storyline: The Sugar Land Skeeters turned in the most dominant offensive performance of the season with 52 runs and 15 home runs, cruising past North Correa 5-4 in a week where the Skeeters looked untouchable at the plate. Meanwhile, the Albuquerque Bombers delivered the most lopsided result of Week 5 with an 8-1 demolition of the Reapers, powered by Kyle Harrison's 15 strikeouts and 1.00 ERA alongside Landen Roupp's 13 K and 0.79 WHIP — the pitching staff was simply unplayable. Together, these two performances signal a widening gap between the league's elite and the teams still searching for identity five weeks in.
lucky_reason: The Benchwarmers own the highest luck delta in the league at +1.33 and managed to steal a 6-4 result despite posting only 3 home runs, a 5.28 ERA, and one of the thinnest power profiles in the league this week.
unlucky_reason: The Wonderdogs carry the worst luck delta in the league at -0.62 and backed it up this week with Harris delivering 9 RBI, Moniak hitting 2 home runs, and the pitching staff posting a solid 4.06 ERA — and still couldn't do better than a 5-5 tie.
records_broken: The Albuquerque Bombers tied the single-week strikeout record with 77 K, matching the Abbotsford-Colby Twins' mark set in Week 2, driven by Kyle Harrison and Landen Roupp combining for 28 strikeouts alone.
power_rankings: 1. Sugar Land Skeeters | 52 runs and 15 HR remain the most jaw-dropping offensive week of the season; this lineup has no clear weakness and the Skeeters have been top-two in category wins every single week | same

2. General Hospital | A frustrating 5-5 tie despite Wrobleski (0.69 ERA) and Fried (0.63 WHIP) carrying the rotation; Kumar Rocker now added to an already elite staff makes this team the most frightening on paper | same

3. Albuquerque Bombers | The 8-1 demolition of the Reapers was the statement win of the week — Harrison and Roupp were unhittable, Jung and Lile added immediate lineup value, and four waiver adds show a manager locked in and building | up

4. Hiroshima Radiation | McLean's 17 K and 12 team NSVH are legitimate category weapons; a 5-5 tie that felt winnable stings, but the saves-and-holds moat is real and this roster is deeper than its record suggests | up

5. BaseballPro77 | Lost 5-3 but Murakami, Trout, and Basallo's .611 OBP all showed up again; the offense is consistently elite and Strahm's addition tightens the bullpen enough to turn close losses into wins | down

6. Athletic Park Dragons | 13 HR and 39 RBI and still lost 6-4 because the rotation posted a 6.31 ERA with zero quality starts — the offensive ceiling is among the league's best but the pitching is a five-alarm fire | down

7. North Correa | Glasnow posted a 0.25 WHIP and Sale a 0.50 WHIP and they still lost 5-4 — that's a brutal pill to swallow; Ohtani's elite multi-category output and 10 team steals keep this roster dangerous week to week | same

8. Riverside Roughnecks | Quietly one of the best stories of the week: beat a loaded Dragons squad with Kurtz (8 R, 3 HR), Suzuki (3 HR), and 5 QS from a rotation that is starting to look like a real strength | up

9. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Aranda's 3 HR and Griffin's dominant outing were encouraging, but a -0.43 luck delta and a reliance on a thin pitching staff means the 5-3 win may flatter this roster a touch | same

10. Canberra Wombats | Raleigh (4 HR) and Walker (3 HR) kept this team alive for a 5-5 tie but Nola's 11.00 ERA and a team ERA of 5.59 with just 2 QS underscore how fragile this rotation has become after dropping Sasaki | down

11. Buttville Skidmarks | De La Cruz and Caminero are a real power tandem and 5 NSVH is a genuine asset, but 24 runs scored and Bradish's 2.03 WHIP leave this roster feeling like it needs another consistent bat to push upward | same

12. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Got blown out 8-1 across nearly every pitching category, and dropping McGreevy without a clear replacement signals a front office without answers; this roster is trending the wrong direction | down

13. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | The -0.62 luck delta is now a recurring identity crisis — Harris, Moniak, and solid pitching all week produced only a tie; Dollander's addition offers rotation hope but the losses are piling up in the standings | same

14. Benchwarmers | A +1.33 luck delta is the most unsustainable number in the league; 3 HR, a 5.28 ERA, and dropping Scherzer and Assad leave this roster with very little margin for error going forward | same
waiver_highlights: Albuquerque Bombers | A | Added Jung (3B), Garrett (SP), Winn (SP,RP), Marte (3B,OF); dropped Antonacci, Loáisiga | Jung immediately contributed 3 R, 1 HR, 3 RBI, and 1 SB in his debut week while Garrett and Winn add rotation depth to what is already the hottest pitching staff in the league — this is the most aggressive and effective waiver week of the season.

General Hospital | A | Added Kumar Rocker (SP); dropped Leiter, Junk | Rocker slides into an already elite rotation featuring Wrobleski and Fried, making General Hospital's pitching staff arguably the scariest in the league and a must-watch storyline heading into Week 6.

Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | B | Added Dollander (SP,RP), Vargas (1B,2B), Bruihl (RP), Miller (RP); dropped Flaherty, Strahm, Sterner, Clarke | Losing Strahm hurts — he was a real bullpen piece — but Dollander is a high-upside SP add that this team desperately needed after weeks of rotation inconsistency; whether the volume of moves signals panic or a plan is worth watching.

Canberra Wombats | D | Added Ritchie (SP); dropped Sasaki (SP) | Dropping Roki Sasaki for JR Ritchie is a stunning roster decision that immediately weakens the most vulnerable part of this team; Nola's 11.00 ERA combined with losing Sasaki could make Week 6 a pitching disaster.

Benchwarmers | C | Added Schmitt (1B,2B,3B); dropped Naylor (1B), Scherzer (SP), Assad (SP) | Losing Scherzer and Assad without meaningful pitching replacements is alarming for a team already sitting on a 5.28 ERA — Schmitt adds positional flexibility but does nothing to address the power and pitching deficiencies that make this roster fragile.

North Correa | B | Added Wells (C); dropped Realmuto (C) | Upgrading from a declining Realmuto to the emerging Austin Wells is smart roster management; Wells offers more upside at the position and keeps North Correa's depth at catcher in good shape heading into a tough Week 6 matchup against Hiroshima.

Riverside Roughnecks | B | Added Bachman (RP), Vodnik (RP); dropped Vientos (1B,3B) | Surrendering Vientos's bat hurts the lineup, but shoring up the bullpen with two live-arm relievers addresses a real need for a team trying to convert quality starts into wins and holds.
